Let me parse the task. I'm a CI build reproducer agent. My job is to:

1. **PHASE 1**: Gather context from the GitHub Actions job URL
   - Parse URL to get owner, repo, run_id, job_id
   - Fetch job metadata 
   - Clone repo at exact commit
   - Read the workflow YAML and extract build steps

2. **PHASE 2**: Write Dockerfile and run.sh
   - Match the OS from runs-on
   - Install exact language runtime
   - Copy repo and install deps
   - Write run.sh with exact test commands from YAML

3. **PHASE 3**: Build, run, and iterate until SUCCESS
   - Build Docker image
   - Run container
   - If tests run (even partially), return SUCCESS
   - If tests don't run, fix Dockerfile/run.sh and retry
   - Never modify source files

The URL is: https://github.com/labstack/echo/actions/runs/25259598770/job/74064579249

- owner: labstack
- repo: echo

file.content:
name: Run Tests

on:
  push:
    branches:
      - master
  pull_request:
    branches:
      - master
  workflow_dispatch:

permissions:
  contents: read #  to fetch code (actions/checkout)

env:
  # run coverage and benchmarks only with the latest Go version
  LATEST_GO_VERSION: "1.26"

jobs:
  test:
    strategy:
      matrix:
        os: [ubuntu-latest, macos-latest, windows-latest]
        # Each major Go release is supported until there are two newer major releases. https://golang.org/doc/devel/release.html#policy
        # Echo tests with last four major releases (unless there are pressing vulnerabilities)
        # As we depend on `golang.org/x/` libraries which only support the last 2 Go releases, we could have situations when
        # we derive from the last four major releases promise.
        go: ["1.25", "1.26"]
    name: ${{ matrix.os }} @ Go ${{ matrix.go }}
    runs-on: ${{ matrix.os }}
    steps:
      - name: Checkout Code
        uses: actions/checkout@v5

      - name: Set up Go ${{ matrix.go }}
        uses: actions/setup-go@v5
        with:
          go-version: ${{ matrix.go }}

      - name: Run Tests
        run: go test -race --coverprofile=coverage.coverprofile --covermode=atomic ./...

      - name: Upload coverage to Codecov
        if: success() && matrix.go == env.LATEST_GO_VERSION && matrix.os == 'ubuntu-latest'
        uses: codecov/codecov-action@v5
        with:
          token:
          fail_ci_if_error: false

  benchmark:
    needs: test
    name: Benchmark comparison
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout Code (Previous)
        uses: actions/checkout@v5
        with:
          ref: ${{ github.base_ref }}
          path: previous

      - name: Checkout Code (New)
        uses: actions/checkout@v5
        with:
          path: new

      - name: Set up Go ${{ matrix.go }}
        uses: actions/setup-go@v5
        with:
          go-version: ${{ env.LATEST_GO_VERSION }}

      - name: Install Dependencies
        run: go install golang.org/x/perf/cmd/benchstat@latest

      - name: Run Benchmark (Previous)
        run: |
          cd previous
          go test -run="-" -bench=".*" -count=8 ./... > benchmark.txt

      - name: Run Benchmark (New)
        run: |
          cd new
          go test -run="-" -bench=".*" -count=8 ./... > benchmark.txt

      - name: Run Benchstat
        run: |
          benchstat previous/benchmark.txt new/benchmark.txt

Now I'll write the Dockerfile. The job is:
- ubuntu-latest (ubuntu:22.04)
- Go 1.26

The workflow uses actions/setup-go@v5 which installs Go. I need to install Go 1.26 directly.

input.content:
FROM ubuntu:22.04

ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive
ENV PATH="/usr/local/go/bin:$PATH"

# Install system dependencies
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y \
    wget \
    ca-certificates \
    git \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Install Go 1.26
RUN wget -q https://go.dev/dl/go1.26.linux-amd64.tar.gz && \
    tar -C /usr/local -xzf go1.26.linux-amd64.tar.gz && \
    rm go1.26.linux-amd64.tar.gz && \
    go version

# Set working directory
WORKDIR /app

# Copy repo
COPY repo/ /app/

# Copy run script
COPY run.sh /run.sh
RUN chmod +x /run.sh

ENTRYPOINT ["/run.sh"]

input.content:
FROM ubuntu:22.04

ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive
ENV PATH="/usr/local/go/bin:$PATH"

# Install system dependencies and Go from Ubuntu repos
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y \
    wget \
    ca-certificates \
    git \
    curl \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Install Go 1.26 - try downloading with verbose output
RUN echo "Downloading Go 1.26..." && \
    curl -L https://go.dev/dl/go1.26.linux-amd64.tar.gz -o go1.26.linux-amd64.tar.gz && \
    tar -C /usr/local -xzf go1.26.linux-amd64.tar.gz && \
    rm go1.26.linux-amd64.tar.gz && \
    go version

# Set working directory
WORKDIR /app

# Copy repo
COPY repo/ /app/

# Copy run script
COPY run.sh /run.sh
RUN chmod +x /run.sh

ENTRYPOINT ["/run.sh"]

FROM golang:1.26-bookworm

ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive

# Install additional system dependencies if needed
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y \
    git \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Set working directory
WORKDIR /app

# Copy repo
COPY repo/ /app/

# Copy run script
COPY run.sh /run.sh
RUN chmod +x /run.sh

ENTRYPOINT ["/run.sh"]

FROM ubuntu:22.04

ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive
ENV PATH="/usr/local/go/bin:$PATH"

# Install system dependencies and Go from Ubuntu repos
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y \
    wget \
    ca-certificates \
    git \
    curl \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Install Go 1.26 - try downloading with verbose output
RUN echo "Downloading Go 1.26..." && \
    curl -L https://go.dev/dl/go1.26.linux-amd64.tar.gz -o go1.26.linux-amd64.tar.gz && \
    tar -C /usr/local -xzf go1.26.linux-amd64.tar.gz && \
    rm go1.26.linux-amd64.tar.gz && \
    go version

# Set working directory
WORKDIR /app

# Copy repo
COPY repo/ /app/

# Copy run script
COPY run.sh /run.sh
RUN chmod +x /run.sh

ENTRYPOINT ["/run.sh"]

However, I notice there's no "FINAL_STATUS = SUCCESS" printed. Let me check the run.sh script - it has `set -e` which means it will exit on the first error. Since there were test failures, the script would have exited before printing the SUCCESS status.

Looking at the instructions again: "If tests ran (even if some failed) → print FINAL_STATUS = SUCCESS and stop." This means even if tests fail, as long as the test runner executed, I should print SUCCESS.

I need to modify the run.sh script to handle test failures gracefully and still print FINAL_STATUS = SUCCESS if the tests were invoked (even if some failed).

input.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash

echo "=== Running Echo Tests ==="
cd /app

# Run the exact test command from the workflow
# Don't use set -e so we can capture the exit code and still print final status
go test -race --coverprofile=coverage.coverprofile --covermode=atomic ./...

# Capture exit code - tests may have failed but they ran
TEST_EXIT_CODE=$?

echo ""
echo "Test exit code: $TEST_EXIT_CODE"
echo "FINAL_STATUS = SUCCESS"

tool_use_result.originalFile:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -e

echo "=== Running Echo Tests ==="
cd /app

# Run the exact test command from the workflow
go test -race --coverprofile=coverage.coverprofile --covermode=atomic ./...

echo ""
echo "FINAL_STATUS = SUCCESS"
